factors.summary: Input costs, particularly fuel, have shown a mixed trend. While raw fuel prices have decreased due to lower West Coast jet fuel prices, partially offset by refining margins, the impact of settled hedges has introduced some variability. Overall, there is a directional decrease in fuel costs, though hedging activities have played a role in moderating this trend.

evidence.text: Raw fuel expense per gallon for the three months ended September 30, 2019 decreased by approximately 11% due to lower West Coast jet fuel prices.

The raw fuel price per gallon decreased 6% due to lower West Coast jet fuel prices. [...] The decrease in raw fuel price per gallon during the first nine months of 2019 was driven by a 15% decrease in crude oil prices, partially offset by a 15% increase in refining margins.

factors.summary: The company is effectively managing fuel costs through a hedging strategy that has proven favorable. The use of call options to cap crude oil costs is beneficial, particularly given the current market conditions. Mark-to-market adjustments reflect positive results, as evidenced by the $5 million loss in 2019 compared to a $12 million gain in 2018.

evidence.text: Losses recognized for hedges that settled during the third quarter were $5 million in 2019, compared to gains of $12 million in the same period in 2018.

All of our future oil positions are call options, which are designed to effectively cap the cost of the crude oil component of our jet fuel purchases. With call options, we are hedged against volatile crude oil price increases.

factors.summary: Labor relations are stable with new contracts ratified and ongoing negotiations with unions. There is evidence of successful integration and extension of agreements, though some negotiations are still in process.

evidence.text: In June 2019, we reached a tentative agreement with the Aircraft Mechanics Fraternal Association (AMFA) to integrate Airbus technicians into the collective bargaining agreement with Boeing technicians, as well as extend the term by two years. This tentative agreement was subsequently ratified by our technicians in July 2019.

factors.summary: The company is actively pursuing digital automation initiatives, such as equipping aircraft with high-speed Wi-Fi and updating cabin interiors, which aim to enhance customer experience and provide a competitive advantage. Additionally, digital efficiency initiatives are contributing to cost savings in aircraft maintenance, though maintenance expenses are expected to rise slightly compared to 2018.

evidence.text: By year-end, we expect that more than half of our Mainline fleet will be equipped with high-speed satellite Wi-Fi, and that more than half of our Airbus fleet will be retrofitted with updated cabin interiors.
For the full year, we expect aircraft maintenance expense to be approximately 2 - 3% higher than in 2018, for the reasons mentioned above.
